program_en: B.S.Sc. in Data Science and Policy Studies
program_chi: 數據科學與政策研究社會科學學士
learning_outcomes:
1 1. Major Programme: Upon completion of their studies, students will have
developed: (1)  |  competency in knowledge of policy-making, data
analytics, machine learning, Internet of Things (IoT), artificial
intelligence, information and communication technologies (ICT), statistics,
engineering and relevant fields appropriate to the degree discipline;
(2)  |  intellectual and professional capacities to generate
actionable social knowledge for deriving data-driven and evidence-based
decisions with policy impacts;
(3)  |  the ability to use big data, computer, statistical and
ICT technologies to conduct research and analysis, and to interpret results
with understandings of their process, limitations and implications;
(4)  |  the ability to integrate and apply knowledge from an
interdisciplinary and transdisciplinary approach to design and implement
policy solutions;
(5)  |  the ability to combine analytical power with design
thinking, creativity and innovation;
(6)  |  the ability to understand the technological,
institutional and human dimensions of the impact of technologies and
innovation on organizations and society;
(7)  |  the ability to understand professional and ethical
responsibilities and social, economic, political, legal, and regulatory
impacts of big data and policy change and innovation;
(8)  |  the ability to communicate data analysis, findings, and
policy decisions to major stakeholders;
(9)  |  the ability to lead and work in teams in a global and collaborative
context;
(10)  |  the understanding of entrepreneurship and citizenship;
(11)  |  an international perspective and a critical global
mind-set; and
(12)  |  the ability of co-learning, co-creation of knowledge,
and life-long learning.
2. Minor Programme: Upon completion of the Minor Programme, students will
have demonstrated an understanding of fundamental issues related to policy
studies and acquired basic skills in data science. They will also have a
sufficient understanding of how to make informed and good policy decisions
based on data science and communicate such decisions to different
stakeholders. As data-driven and evidence-based policymaking can be broadly
applied to different sectors to solve complex social problems, the Minor
Programme can synergize with students’ major studies to enhance their
competitiveness in career development.
study_scheme:
Geography and Resource Management Data
Science and Policy Studies Applicable
to students admitted in 2023-24
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a minimum of 72 units
of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
DSPS1001 and any two courses,
offered by two different Programmes/Departments/Schools, chosen from the
following: ARCH1001, 1002,
COMM1110, 1120, 1150, ECON1210, 1220, 2011, GLSD1001, GPAD1020, 1076, 1077,
GRMD1302, 1401, 1402, PSYC1000, 1630, SOCI1001, 1201, SOSC1001, 1002, SOWK1001,
1113, 1114, URSP1001, 1002
2.  |  Required Courses:
(a)  |  DSPS1003, 1004, 3801, 4801 (capstone course), 4802
(capstone course)  |  15
(b)  |  Resident Study Overseas: Students are required to study overseas for one academic
term in a partner institution and complete at least 9 units of courses
related to Data Science and Policy Studies approved by the Programme
Director. Students who are unable to obtain the required units from partner
institutions must make up the shortfall by completing appropriate courses
at CUHK, subject to the approval of the Programme Director.  |  9
3.  |  Elective Courses[a]:
(a)  |  Methods and Tools Any seven courses from the following: COMM3650#, DSPS2102, 2104, 2201, 2202, 2520, 2730,
2830, 3190, 3202, 3330, 3350, 3790, 3791, 3802, ECON2121#, 3121#, GRMD2105#,
SOCI4201#  |  21
(b)  |  Policy Applications Any six courses from any of the following areas:  |  18
(i)  |  Policy Science and Public Governance DSPS2301, 2320, 2501, 3320, 3501, 3803, 4310,
GPAD2065#, 2085#, 2090#, 2095#, 2160#, 2170#
(ii)  |  Global Relationship DSPS3310, GLSD2401#, 2501#, SOCI3241#
(iii)  |  Human Resources PSYC3360#, SOCI3102#, 3227#
(iv)  |  Social Problems, Deviance, Law and Order ECON3630#, PSYC3370#, SOCI2216#, 3002#, 3204#
(v)  |  Sustainable Smart Cities DSPS3340, GRMD2401#, 2403#, 2501#, 3203#, 3404#, 4401#,
4502#, 4503#, URSP3100#, 3300#, 3400#, 3600#
Total:  |  72
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  DSPS courses at 2000 and above level as well as those
labeled as # will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
2.  |  Students are responsible for the extra costs incurred
in relation to the overseas resident study.
[a]  |  Among the
non-DSPS courses, students can only choose up to three courses under the
same subject area.
